text: The robotic arm lifts the divided inferior mesenteric vein (IMV) pedicle upwards, separating the thin mesentery from the retroperitoneum, revealing the pancreas. The surgeon pushes the pancreas down towards the retroperitoneum, elevating the descending mesocolon anteriorly. The frontal colic ligament and lateral attachments of the splenic flexure are divided, entering the areolar tissue plane. The omental colic ligament is peeled up, mobilizing the transverse colon. The medial mesentery is dissected to the border of the middle colic artery, completing the splenic flexure mobilization using cautery.

The surgeon begins the lateral-to-medial dissection by incising the white line of Toldt, reflecting the descending colon towards the patient's right lower quadrant. The spleen and omentum are visible as the dissection progresses towards the head. The omentocolic attachment is incised to enter the lesser sac, completing the mobilization by dividing the omental colic, splenocolic, lenocolic, and phrenocolic attachments. The transverse colon is mobilized using the third robotic arm, with the stomach visible to the left of the screen.
